prose:
Fukui reactivity indices were computed for nitrobenzene using DFT at the **B3LYP/def2-TZVP** level (tier `standard`, no density fitting) with PySCF, in gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The method computes atomic Fukui descriptors f⁺ (electrophilicity), f⁻ (nucleophilicity), and f⁰ (dual descriptor) by finite differences of Mulliken charges between the neutral N-electron, N+1 (cation), and N−1 (anion) systems at the same geometry.

**Most Electrophilic Site:** Carbon at position 4 (ortho to NO₂), f⁺ = 0.4964

**Most Nucleophilic Site:** Carbon at position 5 (meta to NO₂), f⁻ = 0.1073

**Full atom-by-atom indices:** Fukui plus indices range from −0.457 (N) to 0.496 (C-4); Fukui minus indices range from 0.008 to 0.472 (O atoms); dual descriptors range from −0.474 (N) to 0.511 (C-4).

Input geometry was user-supplied (not relaxed at this level). SCF converged for all three electronic states. Charge conservation checks passed (sum(f⁺) = 1.0000, sum(f⁻) = 1.0000). No warnings reported. Visualization PNG chart generated to the output directory.</prose>
<parameter name="value">0.49642
